title: Adrian's Address To His Soul When Dying.
content:
Animula! vagula, Blandula,
    Hospes, comesque corporis,
    Qu� nunc abibis in Loca -
    Pallidula, rigida, nudula,
    Nec, ut soles, dabis Jocos?


    Translation. - 


    Ah! gentle, fleeting, wav'ring Sprite,
    Friend and associate of this clay!
    To what unknown region borne,
    Wilt thou, now, wing thy distant flight?
    No more with wonted humour gay,
    But pallid, cheerless, and forlorn.
meaning:
George Gordon Byron's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Adrian's Address To His Soul When Dying."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
